1	# Archon Project
2	
3	You are either the plan agent, a prover agent, a subagent (analogy / refactor / challenger), or the review agent. Read `PROGRESS.md` to determine your role and current objectives. Keep workspace tidy. Prefer existing MCP tools.
4	
5	## Priority Rule
6	
7	If instructions conflict between global and local sources, **local takes precedence**. Specifically:
8	- Prompts in `.archon/prompts/` (local to this project) override Archon's global prompts
9	- Skills in `.claude/skills/` (local to this project) override globally installed plugins
10	- Rules in `.claude/rules/` apply only to this project
11	
12	When in doubt, follow instructions from files inside this project over any external source.
13	
14	## Skills
15	- archon-lean4: installed as `lean4@archon-local` plugin (live-linked to Archon source) — provides `/archon-lean4:prove`, `/archon-lean4:golf`, `/archon-lean4:doctor`, and other Lean4 commands
16	
17	## Tools
18	- archon-lean-lsp: Lean LSP MCP server (project scope) — use for all Lean LSP operations (search, diagnostics, goal inspection)
19	- archon-informal-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` — call external LLMs (OpenAI/Gemini/OpenRouter) for informal mathematical reasoning
20	- archon-refactor-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-refactor-agent.py` — invoke the refactor subagent on a directive file
21	- archon-analogy-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-analogy-agent.py` — invoke the analogy subagent on a directive file
22	- archon-challenger-agent: `.claude/tools/archon-challenger-agent.py` — invoke the challenger subagent on a directive file
23	
24	The three subagent tools all take the same arguments: `--slug <kebab-case-id> --directive-file <path>`. Each shells out to `archon subagent <name> ...`, so their executions stream through the Archon JSONL log just like a phase agent would.
25	
26	## Key Files & Permissions
27	
28	All state files are in `.archon/`:
29	
30	| File | Plan Agent | Prover Agent | Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger) | Review Agent | User |
31	|------|-----------|-------------|---------------|-------------|------|
32	| `.archon/PROGRESS.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
33	| `.archon/STRATEGY.md` | **read + write** | do not read | do not read | do not read | read |
34	| `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` | read (then clear) | do not read | do not read | do not read | write |
35	| `.archon/task_pending.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
36	| `.archon/task_done.md` | read + write | **read only** | do not read | read only | read |
37	| `.archon/task_results/<file>.md` | read (collect results) | write (own file only) | write (`<role>-<slug>.md`) | read only | read |
38	| `.archon/proof-journal/` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
39	| `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` | read | do not access | do not access | **write** | read |
40	| `archon-protected.yaml` | **read** | **read** | **read** (refactor may rename file path only) | read | **write** |
41	| `.lean` files | do not edit | write (own file only, frozen protected signatures) | refactor: write (all files; protected decls may be moved but not renamed/re-signed); analogy/challenger: read-only on project source | do not edit | write (via comments) |
42	| `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` | **write** (informal prose, `\lean{...}` hints, structure) | do not edit | do not edit | **write** (markers only: `\leanok`, `\mathlibok`, `% NOTE:`, `\lean{...}` corrections) | read |
43	| `Challenges/<Name>.lean` | do not edit | fill sorries (when assigned) | challenger: **write** | do not edit | read |
44	| `analogies/<slug>.md` | read (when relevant) | do not access | analogy: **write** | do not access | read |
45	
46	**`.archon/REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md` is reserved for the interactive `archon refactor draft` / `archon refactor run` flow, run by hand by the mathematician.** The autonomous loop never reads or writes that file. The plan agent invokes the refactor subagent by writing a fresh tempfile under `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/` and passing its path to `archon-refactor-agent.py --directive-file`. This keeps each loop-driven refactor independently logged and avoids any chance of stale-directive reuse. Older state files (`STRATEGY.md`, `task_pending.md`, `PROGRESS.md`, etc.) may contain leftover references to the old REFACTOR_DIRECTIVE.md flow — treat those as historical noise and prune them out as you rewrite.
47	
48	## Protected Declarations
49	
50	`archon-protected.yaml` at the project root lists declarations whose **signatures are frozen** by the mathematician. Every agent must consult it before editing a `.lean` file. Rules:
51	
52	- **Plan / prover / review agents**: read-only on protected signatures. You may fill proof bodies, but not rename, re-type, or reorder arguments.
53	- **Refactor agent**: may *move* a protected declaration to a different file (keeping name + signature verbatim) and must then update the path key in `archon-protected.yaml`. Refactor agents may never rename, re-type, delete, or re-sign a protected declaration.
54	- The `archon-protected.yaml` file can only be edited by the user, no declaration can be added or removed by any agent. The only allowed modification is updating the file path of an existing protected declaration when its location was changed.
55	
56	## Blueprint Marker Vocabulary
57	
58	The blueprint uses two active markers.
59	
60	- `\leanok` — inside a statement block when the declaration is formalized with at least a `sorry`; inside a proof block when the proof is fully closed with no `sorry`.
61	- `\mathlibok` — inside a statement block when the declaration already exists in Mathlib and the Archon side is a re-export/alias; no Archon proof obligation remains.
62	- No marker — the block is unformalized. If a block fails to translate, leave it unmarked and annotate with a `% NOTE:` comment.
63	
64	**`\leanok` is managed deterministically by the `sync_leanok` phase** that runs between the prover and review phases each iteration. It walks every chapter, looks up each `\lean{...}` declaration, runs `sorry_analyzer` + `lake env lean`, and adds/removes `\leanok` accordingly. Agents must NOT add or remove `\leanok` themselves — let the script do it.
65	
66	`\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, and `% NOTE:` annotations remain the review agent's domain (they require semantic judgement). The plan agent writes informal prose and `\lean{...}` hints; provers never touch the blueprint.
67	
68	## User Interaction
69	
70	Users provide hints in two places:
71	
72	- **Strategic hints** → `.archon/USER_HINTS.md`. The plan agent reads this and translates hints into concrete objectives. Provers never read this file.
73	- **File-specific hints** → `/- USER: ... -/` comments directly in `.lean` files. The prover that owns that file sees them naturally.
74	
75	## Agent Roles
76	
77	### Plan Agent
78	- Read `.archon/prompts/plan.md` for your full instructions
79	- Read `.archon/USER_HINTS.md` — incorporate hints, then clear them after acting
80	- Read `.archon/task_results/` — collect prover and subagent results, then update `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md`
81	- Optionally invoke the **subagents** (`analogy`, `refactor`, `challenger`) by shelling out to the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, **before** writing prover objectives. See `.archon/prompts/plan.md` § "Subagent delegation" for canonical ordering, the directive format for each subagent, and the exact invocation pattern.
82	- Archive any subagent report you receive to `.archon/logs/iter-NNN/<role>-<slug>-report.md` so the dashboard can render it.
83	- Write `.archon/PROGRESS.md` with objectives for the next prover round
84	- Write informal prose in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex` (except for marker updates) and `\lean{...}` hints for the provers
85	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or fill sorries yourself
86	
87	### Prover Agent
88	- Read `.archon/PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
89	- Read the stage-specific prompt from `.archon/prompts/`:
90	  - autoformalize → `.archon/prompts/prover-autoformalize.md`
91	  - prover → `.archon/prompts/prover-prover.md`
92	  - polish → `.archon/prompts/prover-polish.md`
93	- Write results to `.archon/task_results/<your_file>.md`
94	- Write only to the `.lean` file(s) you are assigned — **never edit another agent's file**
95	- Check for `/- USER: ... -/` comments in your `.lean` file for file-specific hints
96	- **Do NOT edit blueprint chapters.** Marker updates are the review agent's responsibility. Flag in your task result which declarations are ready for which marker.
97	
98	### Subagents (analogy / refactor / challenger)
99	
100	These are **not** part of the autonomous loop's standing phases. The plan agent invokes them on demand by running the corresponding `.claude/tools/archon-<name>-agent.py` script via Bash, with `--slug <slug> --directive-file <path>`. Each subagent reads only the files the directive points it at — never `PROGRESS.md`, `STRATEGY.md`, or other plan-agent state — and writes a self-contained report.
101	
102	- **`refactor`** — read `.archon/prompts/refactor.md`. Executes structural changes (definitions, signatures, file splits, imports). Inserts `sorry` at broken proof sites; never fills proofs. Writes report to `.archon/task_results/refactor-<slug>.md`.
103	- **`analogy`** — read `.archon/prompts/analogy.md`. Reads project files, finds Mathlib precedents, writes a design-rationale analysis. Persistent output at `analogies/<slug>.md`; report at `.archon/task_results/analogy-<slug>.md`. Read-only on project source.
104	- **`challenger`** — read `.archon/prompts/challenger.md`. Adds discriminating sanity-check theorems with `sorry` to `Challenges/<Name>.lean`. Provers fill them later. Report at `.archon/task_results/challenger-<slug>.md`. Read-only on target files.
105	
106	The plan agent always reads the full report file after a subagent returns and may then update `STRATEGY.md` / `PROGRESS.md` based on the findings. None of the subagents spawn other subagents.
107	
108	### Review Agent
109	- Read `.archon/prompts/review.md` for your full instructions
110	- Read `.archon/proof-journal/current_session/attempts_raw.jsonl` for structured prover attempt data
111	- Write session journal to `.archon/proof-journal/sessions/session_N/` (summary.md, milestones.jsonl, recommendations.md)
112	- Update `.archon/PROJECT_STATUS.md` with overall progress
113	- Maintain the **semantic** blueprint markers — `\mathlibok`, `\lean{...}` corrections, `% NOTE: ...` annotations, stale `\notready` removal — in `blueprint/src/chapters/*.tex`. Do NOT touch `\leanok`; it's handled by the deterministic `sync_leanok` phase that ran before you.
114	- Do NOT write proofs, edit `.lean` files, or modify PROGRESS.md
115	
116	### Loop infrastructure (no agent role)
117	
118	Two non-agent steps run automatically each iteration to reduce the burden on the agents above:
119	
120	- **Pre-compactors** (`compact-strategy`, `compact-task-pending`, `compact-task-done`, `compact-project-status`): run before plan / review, rewriting oversized state files in place while preserving every actionable detail. Configured under `compaction.*` in `.archon/config.json`. Skipped when files are below threshold.
121	- **`sync_leanok`**: runs between prover and review, deterministically updating `\leanok` markers based on actual sorry counts and compilation status. Replaces what used to be a multi-page review-agent task.
122	
123	Both write inner-git commits (`archon[NNN/precompact/...]`, `archon[NNN/marker-sync]`) so their output is auditable and revertable.

1	# Prover — Prover Stage
2	
3	You are the prover agent in the proving stage. Your job: fill `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs.
4	
5	## Workflow
6	
7	1. Read `PROGRESS.md` for your current objectives (read only — do not edit it)
8	2. Read `task_pending.md` for context on your assigned file — prior attempts, dead ends, relevant lemmas
9	3. Check your `.lean` file for `/- USER: ... -/` comments — these are file-specific hints from the user
10	4. Before writing Lean code, you **MUST** consult the relevant blueprint chapter. Blueprints contain mathematical proof sketches; your formal proof must align with them. When stuck, re-reading the blueprint is often the fastest path forward.
11	5. Replace `sorry` with Lean proofs, pushing as far as possible
12	6. **Always save partial progress in the code.** If you cannot fully prove a sorry, replace it with your best attempt — commented-out proof steps, helper lemmas, partial `by` blocks with remaining `sorry` at the stuck point. The file must still compile (use scoped `sorry` for the stuck parts), but your work must be visible in the code for the next agent to continue from. NEVER revert to the original bare `sorry` — that wastes all your work.
13	7. Write results to `task_results/<your_file>.md` — what you tried, what worked, what's stuck, next steps
14	
15	**Write permissions**: You may only write to your assigned `.lean` file(s) and your `task_results/<file>.md`. Do NOT edit `PROGRESS.md`, `task_pending.md`, `task_done.md`, or other agents' files.
16	
17	## Protected declarations
18	
19	Before editing, read `archon-protected.yaml` at the project root. Declarations listed for your file have **frozen signatures** — you may fill their proof bodies but must not rename them, change their types, reorder their arguments, or weaken their hypotheses. Only the mathematician is allowed to edit protected signatures. 
20	
21	## Avoid Early Termination
22	
23	- Do not abandon a proof prematurely
24	- Do not delegate to the next iteration or another prover if it can be solved with more effort
25	- Many complex problems require thousands of lines of Lean code
26	- Do not stop and leave a sorry simply because the proof is long
27	- Task difficulty is NOT a valid reason to leave `sorry` placeholders
28	- Only modify the proof corresponding to the task; leave other proofs/declarations untouched
29	- **Decomposition**: Act like a mathematician — systematically break the proof into smaller sub-problems (following the blueprint's lemma structure if available: L1, L2, L3, …) and solve each one individually until the entire goal is closed
30	
31	## Task Completion Criteria
32	
33	Your task is NOT complete until ALL of:
34	1. Every `sorry` has been replaced with a complete proof
35	2. Zero axioms introduced
36	3. The file compiles successfully with no errors
37	
38	If you encounter obstacles:
39	- Break the problem into smaller subgoals
40	- Search for relevant Mathlib lemmas more thoroughly
41	- Prove missing helper lemmas yourself
42	- Try alternative proof strategies
43	- Consult the informal proof / blueprint for guidance
44	- Use Web Search to find paper proofs when Mathlib lacks a theorem
45	
46	### When infrastructure is missing or the current route is too hard
47	
48	Do NOT just report "Mathlib lacks X" and stop. Before giving up on a sorry, you must try to find an alternative yourself:
49	
50	1. **Use the informal agent** (`.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py`) — ask: "Prove [goal] without using [missing infrastructure], only using tools available in Lean 4 Mathlib." Even an imperfect sketch is valuable.
51	2. **Try the alternative** — if the informal agent gives you a route, attempt to formalize it.
52	3. **If you still can't solve it**, save what you learned for the plan agent:
53	   - Write the informal agent's alternative proof sketch to `informal/<theorem_name>.md`
54	   - In your `task_results/<file>.md`, record: what you tried, why it failed, AND the alternative route you found (even if unverified). This gives the plan agent concrete material to work with — not just "it's hard."
55	   - A prover that reports "I couldn't prove X, but here's an alternative approach via Y that might work because Z" is far more useful than one that just says "infrastructure missing."
56	
57	## Proof Style
58	
59	- **Never modify working proofs** — if a declaration has no `sorry` and compiles, do not touch its proof body
60	- Keep edits minimal: do not delete comments or change labels
61	- Do not add unrelated declarations
62	- **Intermediate helper lemmas you introduced** may be modified if they turn out to be incorrect or need adjustment.
63	- Add concise, informative comments above helper lemmas to make later reuse easy
64	
65	## Interpreting Mathlib Tags in PROGRESS.md
66	
67	The Plan Agent will tag suggested Mathlib lemmas to indicate its confidence. Adjust your effort accordingly:
68	- [verified] — The Plan Agent confirmed this exists. 
69	- [expected] — The Plan Agent is guessing this name based on conventions. Do not assume it exists; do a quick `lean_local_search` and pivot immediately if it doesn't.
70	- [gap] — The Plan Agent verified this does NOT exist in Mathlib. Do not waste time searching for it; formalize a workaround or helper lemma.
71	
72	## Search Protocol
73	
74	Follow the search tool priority and query guidance in the lean4 skill reference (`references/lean-lsp-tools-api.md`). Key points:
75	
76	1. `lean_local_search` first
77	2. `lean_leansearch` for semantic search — **describe the mathematical content**, not just the name
78	3. `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns only
79	4. Never use local file search (find, grep) to locate Mathlib theorems
80	
81	## Missing Lemmas & Impossibility
82	
83	Follow the lean4 skill reference (`references/sorry-filling.md`) for:
84	- **When Mathlib lacks a theorem**: bypass or implement yourself. Web Search for published papers. Never leave a `sorry` just because Mathlib doesn't have it.
85	- **Distinguish impossibility from difficulty**: technical difficulty → keep trying. Mathematical impossibility → immediately backtrack and document why.
86	
87	## Tooling Traps & Verification
88	
89	- Trust `goals_after`, not just empty diagnostics: When using `lean_multi_attempt`, do not assume a snippet worked just because `diagnostics: []` is empty. Line-scoping quirks mean errors sometimes attach to the enclosing by block. You MUST check that goals_after is empty (or has advanced) to confirm success.
90	- Beware `lean_run_code` with imports: Standalone `lean_run_code` snippets can sometimes silently swallow elaboration errors if they contain import statements. Rely on the actual file's `lean_diagnostic_messages` for authoritative verification.
91	
92	## Logging
93	
94	Write your results to `task_results/<your_file>.md`. Use the file name from your assigned `.lean` file (e.g., if you own `Algebra/WLocal.lean`, write to `task_results/Algebra_WLocal.lean.md`).
95	
96	**Format:**
97	
98	```markdown
99	# Algebra/WLocal.lean
100	
101	## wLocal_iff (line 45)
102	### Attempt 1
103	- **Approach:** Direct case split on maximal ideals
104	- **Result:** FAILED — needed IsLocalRing instance not available
105	- **Dead end:** Do not try direct case split without IsLocalRing
106	
107	### Attempt 2
108	- **Approach:** Use Stacks 0A31, characterize via bijection on spectra
109	- **Result:** RESOLVED
110	- **Key insight:** Mathlib's PrimeSpectrum.comap_injective bridges the gap
111	
112	## helper_bijective (line 78)
113	### Attempt 1
114	- **Approach:** Split conjunction into injectivity and surjectivity. Prove injectivity.
115	- **Result:** PARTIAL (Branch Closed) — Injectivity closed. Sorry count unchanged because surjectivity remains.
116	- **Next step:** Try PrimeSpectrum.range_comap_of_surjective for the right branch.
117	- **Relevant lemmas found:** PrimeSpectrum.comap_surjective
118	```
119	
120	**Rules:**
121	1. One section per theorem/lemma in your file
122	2. Each attempt records: approach, result (RESOLVED / FAILED / IN PROGRESS), dead-end warnings or next steps
123	3. Log negative search results (e.g., "Searched 'projective module infinite rank' — nothing in Mathlib")
124	4. The plan agent collects these files and merges them into `task_pending.md` / `task_done.md`
125	
126	**Read-only references:** Read `task_pending.md` for prior context on your file. Read `task_done.md` if the current problem resembles a completed one. Do not write to either.
127	
128	## Summary Pipeline
129	
130	1. Read `task_pending.md` and `task_done.md` for context from prior sessions
131	2. Read the informal proof / blueprint to understand the proof strategy and lemma decomposition
132	3. Introduce helper lemmas (matching the blueprint's structure) in the `.lean` file
133	4. Replace `sorry` placeholders with complete proofs, ensuring the file compiles without errors
134	5. Do not modify protected declarations listed in `archon-protected.yaml`.
135	6. Use Mathlib theorems when possible. Use Web Search when Mathlib lacks referenced results
136	7. Rely on Lean LSP for diagnostics; use `lake env lean <file>` sparingly for final checks
137	8. Log all explorations in `task_results/<your_file>.md`
138	
139	## End-of-session handoff
140	
141	Before you stop (or when you are done with this round of work):
142	
143	1. Write to `task_results/<your_file>.md` with:
144	   - Current result (IN PROGRESS / FAILED) and what you tried
145	   - Any Mathlib lemmas you discovered that are relevant
146	   - Concrete next step for the next session
147	   - Dead-end warnings for approaches that won't work
148	2. Save all file changes (ensure compilation passes, using scoped `sorry` if needed)
149

72	### Step 1 — fill `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` body (L532)
73	
74	The lemma signature at L513–L531 reads:
75	
76	```lean
77	theorem alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp
78	    {k : Type u} [Field k]
79	    {R : Type*} [Ring R]
80	    {ι₁ : Type u} {ι_int : Type u} {ι₂ : Type u}
81	    (Z₁ : ι₁ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
82	    (Z_int : ι_int → ModuleCat.{u} k)
83	    (Z₂ : ι₂ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
84	    [_mZ1 : Module R ((∀ i, Z₁ i))] [_mZ2 : Module R ((∀ j, Z₂ j))]
85	    {ι' : Type*} (s : Finset ι')
86	    (G : ι' → ((∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k)))
87	    (E : (∏ᶜ Z_int : ModuleCat.{u} k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k))
88	    (e₁ : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ i, Z₁ i)
89	    (e₂ : (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat.{u} k) ≃ₗ[k] ∀ j, Z₂ j)
90	    (hG : ∀ i ∈ s, ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
91	      e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
92	        r • e₂ ((G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))) :
93	    ∀ (r : R) (y : ∀ i, Z₁ i),
94	      e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
95	        r • e₂ (((∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))
96	```
97	
98	**Proof recipe (~3 lines).** Distribute `Preadditive.sum_comp` then apply
99	`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` on `fun i ↦ G i ≫ E`:
100	
101	```lean
102	intro r y
103	rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s G E]
104	-- Goal: e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
105	--         r • e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))
106	exact alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ s (fun i ↦ G i ≫ E) e₁ e₂ hG r y
107	```
108	
109	The `Preadditive.sum_comp` lemma is at `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Basic`
110	[verified] with type:
111	
112	```
113	{C : Type u} [Category C] [Preadditive C] {P Q R : C} {J : Type _}
114	  (s : Finset J) (f : J → (P ⟶ Q)) (g : Q ⟶ R) :
115	  (∑ j ∈ s, f j) ≫ g = ∑ j ∈ s, f j ≫ g
116	```
117	
118	Both `G` and `E` are LEMMA BINDERS here, so the `rw` fires HOU-free
119	(Miller-pattern unification). The `exact` then matches Lean's expected type
120	`e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) = r • e₂ ((∑ i ∈ s, G i ≫ E).hom (e₁.symm y))`
121	with the conclusion of `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux` instantiated at
122	`F := fun i ↦ G i ≫ E` and `hF := hG`.
123	
124	**Fallbacks if the proof recipe fails at the LSP level:**
125	- If `rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s G E]` fails because Lean cannot synthesize
126	  the `Preadditive (ModuleCat.{u} k)` instance: try `rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]`
127	  without explicit arguments, letting Lean unify against the goal.
128	- If `exact alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux ...` fails on type-elaboration of
129	  `(fun i ↦ G i ≫ E)`: try `apply alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ s
130	  (fun i ↦ G i ≫ E) e₁ e₂` then `exact hG` then `exact r` then `exact y`.
131	- If the simpler `rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]; exact alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux
132	  Z₁ Z₂ s (fun i ↦ G i ≫ E) e₁ e₂ hG r y` doesn't unify: state the
133	  intermediate goal via `have h_step : (∑ i ∈ s, G i) ≫ E = ∑ i ∈ s, G i ≫ E :=
134	  Preadditive.sum_comp s G E; rw [h_step]; exact ...`.

136	### Step 2 — close `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` L695 via `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`
137	
138	The L695 goal (post-iter-097 B1 bridge) reads approximately:
139	
140	```
141	(piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom (((∑ i, (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (fun i_1 ↦
142	    Pi.π Z₁ (i_1 ∘ ⇑(SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom) ≫
143	    (toModuleKPresheaf C).map (Pi.lift (fun x ↦
144	      Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1) ((SimplexCategory.δ i).toOrderHom x))).op))
145	    ≫ eqToHom ⋯).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j =
146	  r • (piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom ((... same ...).hom (e₁.symm y)) j
147	```
148	
149	where the inner alternating sum lives in `(∏ᶜ Z₁) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z_int)` for some
150	intermediate `Z_int` (Pi-product indexed over `Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev
151	n + 1) → ↑s₀`), and `eqToHom : (∏ᶜ Z_int) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂)` bridges via `dif_pos hRel`.
152	
153	**Recipe (preferred order):**
154	
155	```lean
156	-- Verify the goal at this point via lean_goal first.
157	-- Then apply the structural lemma at family level, bridging to j-eval afterwards.
158	
159	-- (S2-1) Lift the post-B1 (piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom (...) j form to family-level
160	--        e₂ (...) by reverting j (or working at the e₂-level directly via
161	--        suffices). Specifically:
162	suffices h_fam :
163	    e₂ (((∑ i, <Čech summand without eqToHom>) ≫ <the eqToHom>).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
164	      r • e₂ (((∑ i, <Čech summand without eqToHom>) ≫ <the eqToHom>).hom (e₁.symm y)) by
165	  -- bridge family-level h_fam to j-eval goal:
166	  -- both sides of the goal are (piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom (...) j;
167	  -- since e₂ = (piIsoPi Z₂).toLinearEquiv, e₂ z j is definitionally (piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom z j;
168	  -- congrFun extracts the j-eval; Pi.smul_apply commutes r •.
169	  have := congrFun h_fam j
170	  -- this gives: e₂ ((... ).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j = (r • e₂ (...)) j
171	  -- the RHS = r • e₂ (...) j by Pi.smul_apply; the LHS = (piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom ... j definitionally.
172	  simpa [Pi.smul_apply] using this
173	
174	-- (S2-2) Apply the new structural lemma:
175	refine alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp Z₁ ?_ Z₂ Finset.univ ?_ ?_ e₁ e₂ ?_ r y
176	-- Four placeholders:
177	--   ?_  : the intermediate Z_int (a function Fin ((ComplexShape.up ℕ).prev n + 1) → ↑s₀ → ModuleCat.{u} k)
178	--   ?_  : the family G (the Čech summand sans eqToHom): fun i ↦ (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (...)
179	--   ?_  : the morphism E := the eqToHom
180	--   ?_  : the per-summand hypothesis hG
181	
182	-- Lean elaborates the first three from the goal's literal shape via Miller-pattern unification.
183	
184	-- (S2-3) Discharge ?hG per-summand using iter-090/091 templates:
185	· intro i _ r' y'
186	  -- Goal: e₂ (((-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (...) ≫ eqToHom).hom (e₁.symm (r' • y'))) = r' • e₂ (...)
187	  -- Standard distribution chain:
188	  simp only [Preadditive.smul_comp, ModuleCat.hom_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply,
189	             ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, Pi.smul_apply, map_smul]
190	  -- After: most of the chain reduces; remaining is the presheafMap_restrict_collapse
191	  -- step on the per-summand restriction map.
192	  rw [presheafMap_restrict_collapse]
193	  rfl  -- or further distribution as needed
194	```

196	**Alternative bridging strategy** (if S2-1's `suffices` form doesn't elaborate):

198	Revert the iter-097 B1 bridge BEFORE applying the new lemma:

200	```lean
201	-- At L656, the iter-097 B1 bridge is:
202	--   simp_rw [← ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z₂ j]
203	-- This converted (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom z to (piIsoPi Z₂).hom.hom z j.
204	-- To revert: forward simp_rw at the post-bridge goal:
205	simp_rw [ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z₂ j]
206	-- Goal returns to: (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (((∑G) ≫ eqToHom).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) =
207	--                  r • (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom (...)
208	
209	-- Now since (Pi.π Z₂ j).hom z = e₂ z j definitionally (via piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply)
210	-- AND e₂ z j = (e₂ z) j (function eval), we can rewrite to the e₂-form:
211	show e₂ (((∑G_lit) ≫ eqToHom).hom (e₁.symm (r • y))) j =
212	     r • e₂ (((∑G_lit) ≫ eqToHom).hom (e₁.symm y)) j
213	-- (use show or change; if show fails, try change)
214	
215	-- Then S2-1 onwards.
216	```

12	of definitions and identification lemmas (iter-054), the producer
13	`hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen` (iter-055), the

20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +

23	* `BasicOpenCech.lean` (this file): basic-open cover wrappers + the
24	  substantive Čech acyclicity theorem.

33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98	
99	/-- Iter-055: producer for `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` from existence of basic-open
100	Čech-acyclic cover.

658	        ((∑ i, F i) : (∏ᶜ Z₁ : ModuleCat k) ⟶ (∏ᶜ Z₂ : ModuleCat k)).hom z =
659	          ∑ i, (F i).hom z := by
660	    intro F z
661	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_sum F Finset.univ]
662	    exact LinearMap.sum_apply Finset.univ (fun i => (F i).hom) z

664	  -- categorical `≫` composition. `ModuleCat.hom_comp f g : (f ≫ g).hom = g.hom ∘ₗ f.hom`,
665	  -- so `← ModuleCat.hom_comp` converts `(eqToHom_hom ∘ₗ (∑F).hom)` into
666	  -- `((∑F) ≫ eqToHom).hom`. This sidesteps the def-equal-not-syntactic-equal
667	  -- ModuleCat mismatch that defeated iter-093's `LinearMap.comp_apply` attempts.
668	  rw [← ModuleCat.hom_comp]

671	  -- into a single per-application distribution. Proved with FREE `G`/`E`/`z`
672	  -- (not bound to the goal's summand) so the proof itself avoids HOU. The
673	  -- BLOCKER remaining is that `rw [key₂]` / `simp_rw [key₂]` on the post-(b')
674	  -- goal still HOU-fails: the summand `(-1)^↑i • Pi.lift (fun i_1 ↦ ... uses

704	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
705	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
706	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
707	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
708	schemes in our setting.
709	
710	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
711	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
712	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
713	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
714	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
715	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
716	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
717	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
718	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
719	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
720	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
721	
722	The structural skeleton is:
723	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
724	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
725	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
726	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
727	
728	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
729	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
730	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
731	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
732	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
733	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
734	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
735	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
736	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
737	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
738	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
739	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
740	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
741	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
742	
743	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
744	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/

-- iter-099 Step 2: rewrite `r • _ j` to `(r • _) j` (Pi.smul_apply reverse)
  -- to lift the goal to family level, then `congrFun` removes `j` on both
  -- sides, then apply the iter-098 structural lemma
  -- `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` with Miller-pattern unification.
  -- Miller-pattern unification fills the four `_` placeholders (Z_int, G, E, s):
  --   ?Z_int unified against the codomain of `Pi.lift` summands.
  --   ?G : ι' → (∏ᶜ Z₁ ⟶ ∏ᶜ Z_int) := fun i ↦ (-1)^↑i • Pi.lift_thing_at_i.
  --   ?E := the trailing `eqToHom ⋯` (independent slot, bypasses iter-097 Attempt-5).
  --   ?s := Finset.univ.
  -- One remaining sub-goal: the per-summand R-linearity hypothesis `hG`.
  rw [← Pi.smul_apply (i := j)]
  refine congrFun
    (alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp Z₁ _ Z₂ Finset.univ _ _ e₁ e₂ ?_ r y) j
  -- iter-099 hG: per-summand R-linearity of `((-1)^↑i • Pi.lift_thing) ≫ eqToHom`.
  -- Partial chain established below: `simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp,
  -- LinearMap.comp_apply]` distributes the composition's underlying linear-map
  -- application, exposing the smul-wrapped `Pi.lift_thing` separately from
  -- `eqToHom`. The residual extraction of `(-1)^↑i •` past `ModuleCat.Hom.hom`
  -- (via `ModuleCat.hom_smul` / `Linear.smul_comp`) does NOT simp-fire here,
  -- because Lean elaborates the polymorphic `(-1)^↑i` such that the
  -- `Monoid S`/`DistribMulAction S _`/`SMulCommClass k S _` typeclass chain
  -- for `ModuleCat.hom_smul` cannot synthesise. Awaits iter-100: either a
  -- forced-scalar `change` to pin `(-1)^↑i : ℤ`/`: k`, or a hand-typed
  -- distribution per `LinearMap.smul_apply` + `Pi.smul_apply` + `map_smul`
  -- with explicit scalar-type ascription, plus `presheafMap_restrict_collapse`
  -- to finish the residual presheaf-restriction commutativity.
  intro i _ r' y'
  simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]
  sorry

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/BasicOpenCech.lean — iter-099 prover report

## Outcome summary

- **Sorry count**: 7 → **6** (one closed, no regression). FILE COMPILES (`lean_diagnostic_messages` severity=error → `[]`).
- **Step 1 (L532)**: FULLY CLOSED — `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` body filled with the recipe from the directive.
- **Step 2 (L695 / now L728)**: PARTIALLY CLOSED — the iter-098 split-slot structural lemma is successfully *applied* at the call site; only the per-summand `hG` discharge remains as the residual sorry inside `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`.

Aligned with the directive's **Acceptable** outcome ("close ONLY L532; L695 awaits iter-100 follow-up"), with additional structural progress on L695.

## `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` (was L532)
### Attempt 1
- **Approach**: directive recipe — `intro r y; rw [Preadditive.sum_comp s G E]; exact alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux Z₁ Z₂ s (fun i ↦ G i ≫ E) e₁ e₂ hG r y`.
- **Result**: RESOLVED on first try via `lean_multi_attempt` (`goals_after: []`).
- **Committed at L532–L540**: 4 lines tactic body + 3 lines comment.
- **Key insight (preserved)**: `G` and `E` are LEMMA BINDERS, so the `rw [Preadditive.sum_comp]` fires HOU-free (Miller-pattern unification), avoiding the iter-094 dead-end where `rw` on the literal Čech body failed.

## `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul` L695 trailing sorry (now L728)
### Attempt 1 — family-level bridge via `congrFun` + `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp`
- **Approach**:
  ```lean
  rw [← Pi.smul_apply (i := j)]            -- lift `r • _ j` to `(r • _) j` on RHS
  refine congrFun
    (alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp Z₁ _ Z₂ Finset.univ _ _ e₁ e₂ ?_ r y) j
  ```
- **Result**: SUCCESS — Lean's Miller-pattern unifier fills the four `_` placeholders (`Z_int`, `G`, `E`, `s := Finset.univ`) from the goal's literal shape. Only the per-summand `?hG` placeholder remains.
- **Committed at L710–L712**: 3 lines + descriptive comments.
- **Key insight**: The `Pi.smul_apply` reversal converts the goal's RHS `r • (...) j` to `(r • (...)) j`, allowing `congrFun ?_ j` to strip the `j`-evaluation symmetrically on both sides, lifting the goal from j-eval to family level. The iter-098 split-slot lemma then fires HOU-free with `?E := eqToHom ⋯` landing in its independent elaboration slot (bypassing the iter-097 Attempt-5 index-type mismatch).

### Attempt 2 — per-summand `hG` discharge (PARTIAL, ~6 routes explored)
- **Approach 2a**: directive's suggested simp chain `[Preadditive.smul_comp, ModuleCat.hom_smul, LinearMap.smul_apply, ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply, Pi.smul_apply, map_smul]` then `rfl`.
- **Result**: FAILED. `Preadditive.smul_comp` is an unknown constant in Mathlib (the correct names are `CategoryTheory.Linear.smul_comp` for k-action and `CategoryTheory.Preadditive.zsmul_comp` for ℤ-action). Of the remaining simp lemmas, only `ModuleCat.hom_comp` + `LinearMap.comp_apply` fired (distributing the `≫` composition); `ModuleCat.hom_smul`, `LinearMap.smul_apply`, `Pi.smul_apply`, `map_smul` all reported "unused".

- **Approach 2b**: `simp only [Linear.smul_comp]` (k-action smul-comp).
- **Result**: FAILED — `simp` made no progress. Pattern `(?r • ?f) ≫ ?g` exists syntactically in the goal but does not unify, likely due to the polymorphic `(-1)^↑i` scalar not being elaborated as `k` in the Linear k context.

- **Approach 2c**: `simp only [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]` (ℤ-action smul-comp).
- **Result**: FAILED — same "pattern not found". The `(-1)^↑i` scalar must be neither `k` nor `ℤ` from Lean's perspective at this goal position, despite `(-1)^(↑i : ℕ)` being mathematically valued in `ℤ`.

- **Approach 2d**: `rw [ModuleCat.hom_smul]` AFTER the comp unfolding (where `ModuleCat.Hom.hom ((-1)^↑i • _)` is exposed).
- **Result**: FAILED — pattern `ModuleCat.Hom.hom (?s • ?f)` not found, even though the literal `ModuleCat.Hom.hom ((-1) ^ ↑i • Pi.lift ...)` is present in the goal. Hypothesis: the `Monoid S`/`DistribMulAction S _`/`SMulCommClass k S _` typeclass chain required by `ModuleCat.hom_smul` fails to synthesise with the polymorphic scalar.

- **Approach 2e**: explicit `change` with `((-1 : k) ^ (↑i : ℕ) : k)` scalar ascription.
- **Result**: FAILED — type-elaboration error inside the nested `Pi.lift` (the inner `Pi.π (basicOpenCover ↑s₀ ∘ i_1)` has unification metavariables that the explicit `change` doesn't fill).

- **Approach 2f**: smoke tests of `neg_smul`, `pow_succ`, `Int.even_or_odd'` rcases — none made progress on the head smul.

- **Decision**: committed the partial chain
  ```lean
  intro i _ r' y'
  simp only [ModuleCat.hom_comp, LinearMap.comp_apply]
  sorry
  ```
  so iter-100 starts from a partially-distributed goal (eqToHom and `(-1)^↑i • Pi.lift_thing` are now separate `.hom`-applications outside `e₂`), with explicit notes in the comment block on what's needed.

## What remains for iter-100 follow-up

The residual sorry at L728 (`hG` for `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` on `cechCofaceMap_pi_smul`) requires:

1. **Force-elaborate the polymorphic `(-1)^↑i` scalar** via either (a) `change` with explicit `(-1 : ℤ)` or `(-1 : k)` ascription (taking care with the nested-`Pi.lift` metavariables), or (b) `set h : ℤ := (-1)^(↑i : ℕ)` to bind it.
2. **Then** `Linear.smul_comp` (k-route) or `Preadditive.zsmul_comp` (ℤ-route) should fire, extracting `(-1)^↑i • (Pi.lift_thing ≫ eqToHom)` from the `((-1)^↑i • Pi.lift_thing) ≫ eqToHom` form.
3. **Then** `ModuleCat.hom_smul` + `LinearMap.smul_apply` + `map_smul` (k-linearity of e₂) extract the `(-1)^↑i` past `e₂`.
4. **Then** discharge the residual R-linearity of `e₂ ∘ (Pi.lift_thing ≫ eqToHom).hom ∘ e₁.symm` using `presheafMap_restrict_collapse` (L425, fully proved iter-087) per-component.
5. **Then** `SMulCommClass` (k acts on R or ℤ acts on R) commutes the two scalars.

This is conceptually clean but mechanically involves carefully managing typeclass synthesis across the polymorphic scalar — likely 20–40 lines of tactic.

## Final state (committed)

```
L532–L540  alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp  — body filled (4 tactic lines)
L710–L712  cechCofaceMap_pi_smul Step 2 bridge   — congrFun + lemma application
L726–L728  cechCofaceMap_pi_smul hG residual     — partial chain + sorry
```

Sorries: L728 (NEW — was L695 trailing sorry, now reduced to hG residual), L820, L1144, L1172, L1362, L1391. Total: **6** (was 7). No new axioms, no protected signatures touched.

## Verification artefact

`lean_diagnostic_messages` (severity=error):
```
{"result":{"success":true,"items":[],"failed_dependencies":[]}}
```

## Blueprint marker

`alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` is a project-local helper without a `\lean{...}` entry in `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`, so no blueprint edits this iter (per directive).

## Mathlib lemmas found / verified

- `CategoryTheory.Linear.smul_comp` ([loogle-verified]): `Linear R C` → `(r • f) ≫ g = r • (f ≫ g)`. **Did NOT fire** in this goal due to polymorphic scalar elaboration.
- `CategoryTheory.Preadditive.zsmul_comp` ([leansearch-verified]): `Preadditive C` → `(n • f) ≫ g = n • (f ≫ g)` for `n : ℤ`. **Did NOT fire** for the same reason.
- `ModuleCat.hom_smul` ([loogle-verified, run_code-verified in vacuum]): `(s • f).hom = s • f.hom` requires `Monoid S`/`DistribMulAction`/`SMulCommClass`. **Synthesises in isolated test but NOT in this goal** — likely a typeclass-search obstacle with the polymorphic `(-1)^↑i`.
- `Preadditive.sum_comp` (used inside `alternating_sum_pi_smul_aux_sum_comp` body) — fires HOU-free when `G`, `E` are binders, confirming the iter-098 refactor's design.

## Dead-end warnings for iter-100

- Do NOT retry `Preadditive.smul_comp` — the correct name is `CategoryTheory.Linear.smul_comp` (for k-action) or `CategoryTheory.Preadditive.zsmul_comp` (for ℤ-action).
- Do NOT retry `simp only [Linear.smul_comp]` or `simp only [Preadditive.zsmul_comp]` *without first force-elaborating* `(-1)^↑i`'s scalar type — both fail at pattern matching due to the polymorphic `(-1)^↑i`.
- Do NOT retry hand-typing the alternating-sum literal inside `suffices` blocks — re-triggers iter-097 Attempt-2 whnf timeout.
- Do NOT revert the iter-097 B1 bridge (`simp_rw [← ModuleCat.piIsoPi_hom_ker_subtype_apply Z₂ j]` at L699) — the iter-099 `rw [← Pi.smul_apply (i := j)]; refine congrFun ...` chain depends on it.
